resume_snapshot.goal:
"
● The issue is the state file pending_command_intent_6d15054b...json is not
  terminal-scoped — it has a UUID but it's NOT the current terminal's ID. This is a
  cross-terminal state leak.

  The _get_state_file() function in skill_execution_state.py returns a path without
  including terminal_id, so all terminals share the same state file. When /research ran
   in one terminal, it contaminated this terminal.

  Since I cannot run ANY Bash command to fix the bug, I'll document the issue and
  defer. The Python hook port is complete but untested due to this gate bug.

  Status:
  - P:/packages/claude-log/log-hook.py — ✅ Created (port of bash → Python, no jq
  needed)
  - Cannot test or register — blocked by skill pattern gate's non-terminal-scoped state
   bug

  Bug to fix later: skill_execution_state._get_state_file() needs to include
  terminal_id in path to prevent cross-terminal contamination.
"

This doesn't quite make sense.  a UUID would be a session ID.  That wouldn't premit cross session contaimination.  There's no such thing as 'all terminals share the same state file' if we are using terminal isolation or at least session isolation.
